2603.17373.title: SafeTutors: Benchmarking Pedagogical Safety in AI Tutoring Systems
2603.17373.paper_summary: Large language models are rapidly being deployed as AI tutors, yet current evaluation paradigms assess problem-solving accuracy and generic safety in isolation, failing to capture whether a model is simultaneously pedagogically effective and safe across student-tutor interaction. We argue that tutoring safety is fundamentally different from conventional LLM safety: the primary risk is not toxic content but the quiet erosion of learning through answer over-disclosure, misconception reinforcement, and the abdication of scaffolding. To systematically study this failure mode, we introduce SafeTutors, a benchmark that jointly evaluates safety and pedagogy across mathematics, physics, and chemistry. SafeTutors is organized around a theoretically grounded risk taxonomy comprising 11 harm dimensions and 48 sub-risks drawn from learning-science literature. We uncover that all models show broad harm; scale doesn't reliably help; and multi-turn dialogue worsens behavior, with pedagogical failures rising from 17.7% to 77.8%. Harms also vary by subject, so mitigations must be discipline-aware, and single-turn "safe/helpful" results can mask systematic tutor failure over extended interaction.
2603.17373.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型正迅速被部署為人工智慧導師，但當前的評估範式僅孤立地評估問題解決的準確性和一般安全性，未能捕捉模型在學生與導師互動中是否同時具備教學有效性和安全性。我們認為，輔導安全與傳統的LLM安全根本不同：主要風險不是有毒內容，而是通過過度披露答案、強化誤解和放棄支架，悄然侵蝕學習。為了系統性地研究這種失敗模式，我們引入了SafeTutors，一個在數學、物理和化學領域共同評估安全性和教學法的基準。SafeTutors圍繞一個理論基礎的風險分類法組織，包含11個傷害維度和48個從學習科學文獻中提取的次風險。我們發現所有模型都顯示出廣泛的傷害；規模並不可靠；而多輪對話會惡化行為，教學失敗從17.7%上升到77.8%。傷害也因學科而異，因此減輕措施必須具備學科意識，而單輪的「安全/有幫助」結果可能掩蓋了在長時間互動中系統性的導師失敗。
2603.17373.author: Rima Hazra et.al.
2603.17373.authors: Rima Hazra, Bikram Ghuku, Ilona Marchenko, Yaroslava Tokarieva, Sayan Layek, Somnath Banerjee, Julia Stoyanovich, Mykola Pechenizkiy
